next_steps: Perform rapid plasma reagin (RPR) and confirmatory treponemal antibody testing
Perform HIV testing and screening for other sexually transmitted infections
Obtain skin biopsy of the rash and perform histologic evaluation with silver stain
Check serum complement levels (C3, C4) and ANA/ENA panel
Perform complete metabolic panel, repeat liver function tests, and assess for proteinuria
Consult infectious disease and dermatology specialists
Perform RPR and treponemal antibody testing (EIA or TP-PA)
Screen for HIV, Hepatitis B, and Hepatitis C
Obtain skin biopsy of the rash for histologic evaluation and immunohistochemical staining for Treponema pallidum
Measure serum ferritin, ANA, RF, anti-CCP, and ANCA levels
Perform a complete metabolic panel and check for proteinuria
Refer for ophthalmology evaluation to assess for uveitis or episcleritis
Consult infectious disease and rheumatology specialists
